stories.title: Scottish Open Preview — Scheffler, McIlroy, Rahm and a Defending Champion Headline Renaissance Club Field

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
The Scottish Open field dropped and it's stacked.

Scheffler. McIlroy. Rahm. Fitzpatrick. Fleetwood.

And the defending champion? Chris Gotterup — who won at John Deere on Sunday.

Renaissance Club. North Berwick. First tee 8:43 AM ET Thursday. 🏴󠁧󠁢󠁳󠁣󠁴󠁿⛳

#ScottishOpen

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Jon Rahm is playing the Scottish Open for the first time since 2022.

Koepka and Reed are also in the field after returning from LIV.

$9M on the line at Renaissance Club. One week before Royal Birkdale. 🔥

#ScottishOpen

facebook.long_form:
The Genesis Scottish Open begins Thursday at Renaissance Club in North Berwick, Scotland — and the field might be the strongest the event has ever seen.

Scottie Scheffler, Rory McIlroy, Jon Rahm, Matt Fitzpatrick, and Tommy Fleetwood are all teeing it up, alongside defending champion Chris Gotterup. Gotterup won the John Deere Classic on Sunday — his third win of the 2026 season — and arrives in Scotland as arguably the hottest player on either tour.

Rahm's appearance is notable: it's his first Scottish Open since 2022, and it signals that he's treating this event as serious Open Championship preparation. Brooks Koepka and Patrick Reed are also in the field, having both returned from LIV Golf earlier this year.

This is a $9 million Rolex Series event, co-sanctioned by the PGA Tour and DP World Tour, and it serves as the last competitive tuneup before The Open Championship at Royal Birkdale (July 16-19). Round 1 begins Thursday — Scheffler, Fitzpatrick, and Fleetwood are grouped together for an 8:43 AM ET first-round tee time.

The defending champion coming in on the back of a Sunday win. Rahm returning to Scotland after a three-year absence. McIlroy on home turf. This is must-watch golf.
facebook.image_caption: The Scottish Open field is stacked — Scheffler, McIlroy, Rahm, and defending champion Chris Gotterup all at Renaissance Club. First tee Thursday. 🏴󠁧󠁢󠁳󠁣󠁴󠁿⛳ #ScottishOpen

article.headline: Scheffler, McIlroy, Rahm, and a Defending Champion Who Just Won on Sunday — Scottish Open Field Is Stacked

stories.title: Amundi Evian Championship Preview — Korda's Career Grand Slam Bid Begins Thursday

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Nelly Korda has won 2 of the 5 LPGA majors in 2026.

She tees up at the Evian Championship Thursday — the one major she's never won.

A career Grand Slam is within reach.

Odds: +300 to win. 🏆

#EvianChampionship #LPGA

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Grace Kim won the Evian Championship in 2025 and is back to defend.

Nelly Korda, Minjee Lee, Lydia Ko, and Rose Zhang are all in the field.

$9.1M purse. Evian-les-Bains, France. Thursday through Sunday. 🇫🇷⛳

#EvianChampionship

facebook.long_form:
Nelly Korda arrives at the Amundi Evian Championship this week having already won two of the five LPGA majors in 2026 — the Chevron Championship and the US Women's Open. One more major win would put her one step closer to completing the career Grand Slam. The Evian Championship is one of the two she's still missing.

The tournament is held at Evian Resort Golf Club in Evian-les-Bains, France — the only women's major staged in continental Europe. The purse is $9.1 million, and the field includes defending champion Grace Kim, Minjee Lee, Lydia Ko, and Rose Zhang.

Korda enters as the clear favorite at +300 odds. Ryu Haeran won the KPMG Women's PGA Championship earlier this month, preventing Korda from a clean calendar Grand Slam run in 2026 — but the career Grand Slam remains very much achievable. She needs Evian and the AIG Women's British Open.

First round begins Thursday in France.
facebook.image_caption: Nelly Korda is two majors into 2026 — and the Evian Championship starts Thursday. The career Grand Slam is in play. 🏆🇫🇷 #EvianChampionship #LPGA

article.headline: Nelly Korda Has Won Two Majors in 2026. The Evian Championship Is the One She Needs Next.

research.facts:

**Sources researched:** LPGA Tour official, Golf Channel, ESPN W, Golf Digest
**Key Facts:**

- Venue: Evian Resort Golf Club, Evian-les-Bains, France
- Purse: $9.1 million
- One of five LPGA major championships
- Nelly Korda is odds-on favorite at +300 to win
- Korda won the Chevron Championship (first major of 2026) and the US Women's Open (second major of 2026)
- If Korda wins the Evian Championship, she would complete the career Grand Slam (missing only the AIG Women's British Open, Evian, and KPMG — she still needs Evian and AIG Women's British)
- Actually: Korda won Chevron + US Women's Open in 2026; needs Evian + AIG Women's British Open to complete career Grand Slam; hasn't won those yet
- Ryu Haeran won the 2026 KPMG Women's PGA Championship — stopping Korda's calendar Grand Slam bid for 2026 but Korda can still win career Grand Slam by winning Evian + AIG Women's British Open
- Defending Evian champion: Grace Kim (won 2025 Evian Championship)
- Grace Kim is in the field
- Minjee Lee, Lydia Ko, and Rose Zhang also in the field
- The Evian Championship is the only women's major held in continental Europe
- First round begins Thursday, July 9

stories.title: Open Championship Last-Chance Qualifier — Wesley Bryan Among 12 Chasing Final Spot at Royal Birkdale

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Wesley Bryan is one of 12 players competing for the final spot in The Open Championship.

18 holes. 1 ticket. Royal Birkdale. July 13.

The LCQ is always one of golf's most dramatic days. 🏴󠁧󠁢󠁥󠁮󠁧󠁿⛳

#TheOpen #OpenChampionship

facebook.long_form:
Twelve golfers. Eighteen holes. One spot in The Open Championship.

The Last-Chance Qualifier for the 2026 Open Championship takes place July 13 at Royal Birkdale — three days before the championship itself begins at the same course. The format is unforgiving: 18-hole stroke play, one player earns the final exemption into the field.

Wesley Bryan — PGA Tour member and one of golf's most recognizable social media personalities — is among the 12 competing. Bryan generated controversy earlier this year when he was barred from a PGA Tour pro-am after participating in a LIV Golf influencer event. He hasn't played in The Open Championship before in his career.

Also in the field: Aldrich Potgieter, the young South African power-hitter who plays on the LIV Golf circuit and has drawn attention for his length and shotmaking ability.

Eighteen holes for a spot at a major. Few moments in professional golf are more pressure-compressed than this.
facebook.image_caption: 18 holes. 12 players. 1 spot at The Open. Wesley Bryan is in the field at Royal Birkdale. July 13. ⛳🏴󠁧󠁢󠁥󠁮󠁧󠁿 #TheOpen #OpenChampionship

article.headline: 18 Holes, 12 Players, One Spot: Wesley Bryan Is in the Open Championship Last-Chance Qualifier at Royal Birkdale

- Field: 12 players competing for 1 spot

- Aldrich Potgieter — young South African golfer on LIV Golf roster — also in the LCQ field
- The qualifier takes place three days before the championship begins
- Bryan has not played in The Open Championship previously in his career
- 11 other players in the 12-man field include various DP World Tour and Challenge Tour members

stories.title: LIV Golf CEO Won't Guarantee Final Four Events — Running on Saudi Loans With $400M Shortfall

x_posts.label: Post 4-1
x_posts.text:
LIV Golf CEO Scott O'Neil admitted he cannot guarantee the final 4 events of 2026 will happen.

The league has a $400M shortfall and has been running on Saudi loans.

New Orleans was already postponed. Indianapolis and Michigan are next at risk.

#LIVGolf

x_posts.label: Post 4-2
x_posts.text:
PIF has invested $6 billion into LIV Golf since 2022.

They're signaling they'll stop direct support after this year.

No broadcast deal. No title sponsor. No guaranteed schedule.

The math isn't working. ⛳

#LIVGolf

facebook.long_form:
LIV Golf CEO Scott O'Neil said this week that he cannot guarantee the league's final four events of 2026 will be played.

The UK event and Bedminster (New Jersey) are expected to proceed. But Indianapolis and Michigan are now officially in doubt. New Orleans had already been postponed earlier in the year.

LIV's financial situation has deteriorated significantly. The league has been running on loans from Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund (PIF) — recorded in debenture filings — and faces an estimated $400 million shortfall. PIF has signaled it will wind down direct support after 2026, raising questions about what LIV Golf looks like, or whether it exists at all, in 2027.

The cumulative Saudi investment in LIV Golf since its 2022 launch is estimated at over $6 billion. Without a broadcast deal or title sponsor generating meaningful revenue, that math has never worked — and now the calendar is paying the price.

The sport has changed significantly since LIV launched. Several high-profile players have returned to the PGA Tour. The merger framework between the tours remains unresolved. And the league that was supposed to reshape golf is now struggling to finish its own schedule.
facebook.image_caption: LIV Golf CEO Scott O'Neil can't guarantee the final four events of 2026. A $400M shortfall and Saudi loan dependency are catching up with the league. ⛳ #LIVGolf

article.headline: LIV Golf CEO Can't Guarantee the Final Four Events Will Happen — The $400M Shortfall Is Real

research.facts:
LIV Golf CEO Won't Guarantee Final Four Events — Running on Saudi Loans With $400M Shortfall
**Sources researched:** Sports Business Journal, Bloomberg, Golf Channel, ESPN
**Key Facts:**
- LIV Golf CEO Scott O'Neil stated he cannot guarantee the final four events of the 2026 schedule will be played
- UK event and Bedminster (New Jersey) events are expected to proceed
- Indianapolis and Michigan events are at financial risk
- New Orleans LIV Golf event was already postponed earlier in 2026
- LIV Golf's financial structure: funded by PIF (Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund) loans via debenture filings
- Documented $400 million shortfall in LIV Golf's financials
- PIF has signaled it will end direct support for LIV Golf after 2026
- Cumulative Saudi investment in LIV Golf: estimated $6 billion+ since 2022 launch
- O'Neil replaced Greg Norman as CEO in 2023
- The financial crisis is creating uncertainty for players, caddies, and event venues
- LIV Golf launched in 2022 with 48-player field, shotgun starts, 54-hole format
- Several players have returned to PGA Tour (Brooks Koepka, Patrick Reed)

stories.title: The 2026 PGA Tour Player of the Year Race — Gotterup, Fitzpatrick, and Clark Are Challenging Scheffler's Crown

x_posts.label: Post 5-1
x_posts.text:
The 2026 PGA Tour Player of the Year race is wide open.

Gotterup: 3 wins (4 in last 12 months)
Fitzpatrick: 3 wins
Clark: 2 wins (US Open)

Scheffler: 1 win — but still No. 1 in the world.

Can anyone take the award from him?

#PGATour

x_posts.label: Post 5-2
x_posts.text:
Scottie Scheffler has won 3 straight PGA Tour Player of the Year awards.

Chris Gotterup just won his 3rd tournament of 2026 on Sunday at John Deere.

The case is building. The vote happens at season's end.

This race isn't over. 🏆

#PGATour

facebook.long_form:
Scottie Scheffler has won the PGA Tour Player of the Year award three consecutive times — 2023, 2024, and 2025. But the 2026 race looks different.

Scheffler remains world No. 1 and won the Masters earlier this year. But with only one win on the season, the field has caught up. Three players have built compelling cases:

**Chris Gotterup** leads all PGA Tour players with three wins in 2026 (John Deere Classic, and two earlier events). He's won four times in the last 12 months and is now ranked world No. 4. He arrived at the Scottish Open as the defending champion and the hottest player in the game.

**Matt Fitzpatrick** also has three wins in 2026 — the Valspar Championship, the RBC Canadian Open, and the Zurich Classic (with a partner). He's ranked No. 6 in the world.

**Wyndham Clark** has two wins: the US Open and the CJ Cup Byron Nelson. A major title gives his case significant weight.

Golf Channel ran segments on July 7-8 covering this exact race. The vote doesn't happen until the season ends — but the scoreboard right now suggests this year's award is genuinely up for grabs.
facebook.image_caption: Scheffler has won 3 straight PGA Tour POTY awards — but in 2026, Gotterup (3 wins), Fitzpatrick (3 wins), and Clark (US Open) are making their cases. 🏆 #PGATour

article.headline: Gotterup Has Three Wins. Fitzpatrick Has Three Wins. Clark Won the US Open. Can Any of Them Take the POTY Award From Scheffler?

research.facts:
The 2026 PGA Tour Player of the Year Race — Gotterup, Fitzpatrick, and Clark Are Challenging Scheffler's Crown
**Sources researched:** PGA Tour official, Golf Channel, ESPN Golf, Golf Digest
**Key Facts:**
- Scottie Scheffler is the reigning PGA Tour Player of the Year (3 consecutive awards 2023-2025)
- Scheffler has only 1 win in 2026 (Masters), compared to 4 wins in 2024
- Chris Gotterup: 3 wins in 2026 season (John Deere Classic July 6 was most recent)
- Gotterup has 4 wins in the last 12 months — most of any PGA Tour player
- Matt Fitzpatrick: 3 wins in 2026 (Valspar Championship, RBC Canadian Open, Zurich Classic)
- Wyndham Clark: 2 wins in 2026 (US Open, CJ Cup Byron Nelson)
- Golf Channel ran a segment on July 7-8 on the Player of the Year race
- PGA Tour POTY voting occurs at end of season
- Gotterup is ranked world No. 4 after John Deere win
- Fitzpatrick world No. 6
- Clark world No. 8
- Scheffler remains world No. 1 despite limited 2026 wins
- FedEx Cup standings will also factor into POTY discussion
